type: standup
tags: [standup]
owner: Mike
created: 2026-05-04
status: current
---
### Summary

Meeting discussions focused on contract negotiations, development progress updates, and strategies for addressing customer adoption challenges.  
  
Contract and Development Updates  
Negotiations for customer support contracts focus on mitigating exit fee risks. Development teams are finalizing case load features and provisioning platforms.  
  
Operational and Security Improvements  
Teams are organizing inventory and implementing restricted database user access for better security. Onboarding processes are moving toward shared email inboxes.  
  
Regulatory Wins and Challenges  
New York approval for the remote monitoring platform marks a major milestone. The team decided to develop a resident FAQ template to address guardian apprehension.

  
  

### Next steps

- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Update Email: Change Forward Vessel email to Halo email address.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Michael Weaver] Test Tablet: Test the finalized tablet update functionality this week.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron] Tablet Testing: Reach out to Brian to ensure testing of the tablet update is completed.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron] Implement App Restart: Drive work on restarting applications on the hub from the platform.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Christopher Baron] Research Cellular Backup: Start earnest investigation into the cellular backup option this week.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Define Archiving Process: Work through operational details for handling device archiving and unattended rooms.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Define Ticket Requirements: Define requirements for updating customer ticket options related to move-in and move-out dates.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Michael Weaver] Update Ticket Options: Implement changes for customer ticket options based on requirements received.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Share Install Sheet: Share the sheet outlining the line of sight for upcoming customer installs this week.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Review PRV: Send the resident tasks PRV to the team for feedback and review.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Create Shared Inbox: Create the onboarding shared inbox and add all team members to it.
    
- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    [Shivani Patel] Contact Shiftability: Follow up with Shiftability to gather information on guardian communication and FAQ document content.
    

  
  

### Details

- General Updates and Home Maintenance: Shivani Patel and Michael Weaver briefly discussed personal updates, noting the shift in adulthood toward home maintenance, with Michael Weaver mentioning they had done yard work. Shivani Patel also mentioned needing to update a contact's email from their old "vessel" email to their "halo" email.
    
- Development Progress for Kicking Feature: Michael Weaver provided an update on a specific development task, noting they were close to finishing a "kicking thing" for Shivani Patel. Michael Weaver indicated that the work has been good, involving a lot of learning, and encouraged Shivani Patel to start testing it as soon as it is complete.
    
- Customer Support Contract Negotiations: Shivani Patel explained the extensive contract review process for securing customer support services through a family connection. The provider is a major billing and coding platform for hospital systems, and the contracts are heavily detailed due to dealing with sensitive information like PHI and HIPPA.
    
- Contract Nuances and Capacity Risk: A significant concern in the contract negotiations was ensuring the company would not be charged a large exit fee if they failed to meet the high volume capacity requirements set for the vendor's typical customers. Shivani Patel confirmed that they, along with Brian and Amit, were reviewing the contract details to avoid being stuck with a potential $10,000 bill, noting that they are the smallest company the vendor is taking on.
    
- Team Co-location and Inventory Management: Christopher Baron and Ritwik Rastogi joined the meeting, confirming they were co-located in Columbus. Shivani Patel mentioned planning to come in tomorrow at 10:30 to organize inventory, as they believe a gacha is missing due to finals.
    
- Development Recap and Upcoming Tasks (Michael Weaver): Michael Weaver summarized the previous week's progress, stating that case loads are nearing completion and should be finished this week, and the intercom feature is also close to done, pending review comments from Christopher Baron. They also mentioned addressing a couple of bugs, including one Ritwik Rastogi is reviewing, and the alert details cleanup will be launched today. Michael Weaver's next task will be looking into the ad agency status update.
    
- Development Recap and Upcoming Tasks (Ritwik Rastogi): Ritwik Rastogi reported completing the provisioning checklists for the onboarding side, enabling the management of common areas and residents directly from there. This week, Ritwik Rastogi will focus on the provision app to establish a provisioning platform and integrate the checklists.
    
- Onboarding Process Improvement and Communication: Shivani Patel confirmed that the current onboarding process is a manual step for creating the provisioning checklist after the customer submission. To improve awareness of new customer submissions, Shivani Patel committed to setting up the onboarding email address as a shared group inbox and adding the team, and requested that the system be configured to send an email notification upon form submission.
    
- Inventory and Hardware Updates: Ritwik Rastogi confirmed bringing in two boxes of tablet stands, and Shivani Patel noted that the Reolink hardware is still delayed but expected within a week. Shivani Patel plans to bring in the paper cutter the next morning.
    
- Security, Hub Capabilities, and Backlog Cleanup (Christopher Baron): Christopher Baron announced adding a new, more restricted database user for the case load schedule to improve security. They plan to support Michael Weaver and Ritwik Rastogi, and will start working on functionality to restart apps on the hub from their platform and investigate cellular backup options. Christopher Baron also raised a bug where archiving a smoke detector automatically creates a new one, and proposed fixing this during the current sprint.
    
- Resident Move-In/Out Tracking and New Feature Commitments: Shivani Patel discussed the need for better tracking of resident move-in/out dates, suggesting that a customer support ticket should be created for this process. Shivani Patel committed to passing the requirements for updating customer ticket options to Michael Weaver this week.
    
- Regulatory Approvals and Internal Roadmap: Shivani Patel shared that New York has officially approved Halo as a legal remote monitoring platform, which is a major win for Medicaid reimbursement. They also committed to sharing a sheet with line-of-sight installation dates and plans to finish the resident tasks PRV by the end of the week for review and prioritization in the next cycle.
    
- Customer Acquisition and Adoption Challenges: Shivani Patel provided updates on two C3 customers starting onboarding documentation, estimating a 30-day window between form completion and installation. A key challenge noted was the psychological hurdle of resident and guardian comfort with technology replacing in-person staff, as evidenced by a JCDS resident who obtained a doctor's note requiring constant in-person staff.
    
- Strategy for Guardian Communication: The team discussed the difficulty in addressing guardian apprehension, noting that the fear is more about not having a person present than the technology itself. Michael Weaver expressed confidence that the core issue is the concern about replacing a person with a camera and what happens during an emergency.
    
- Proposed Resident FAQ Solution: Ritwik Rastogi suggested creating a Resident FAQ template with common questions, which agencies could modify with their specific operational details, thus allowing the assurance to come from the agency. Shivani Patel expressed support for this idea, believing it could be quickly turned around and would help the agencies articulate the benefits and emergency response plans.
    
- JCDS Site Reductions and Political Obstacles: Shivani Patel clarified that the JCDS sites were reduced from three to one because guardians for two sites obtained doctor's notes, which is seen as a political move rather than a necessity based on high resident risk. This creates a tricky situation where the agency would incur double costs by paying for both remote support and an in-person staff member.
    
- Future Use Cases and Technical Challenges: Shivani Patel noted that upcoming New York installs will be challenging because they involve apartments with shared Wi-Fi, not a dedicated router per site, which will push the team to navigate how to handle hubs and Reolink connectivity across different units. Christopher Baron confirmed that this is an important area for development that will prepare them for future similar installations.
    
- New York Installation Timeline: Shivani Patel confirmed that current installation estimates are between June and July, with the plan to complete all four New York sites in a single, potentially week-long trip .
